sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check state
after = current_frame.segmentation
print("=== M squares ===")
for n in after['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'M' and n['pixels'] <= 10:
        print(f"  M {n['pixels']}px boundary={n['boundary']}")

print("\n=== Blue shape ===")
for n in after['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'b':
        print(f"  b {n['pixels']}px")

ascii_after = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print(f"\nBottom bar white count: {ascii_after[63].count('W')}")

print(f"\nLevel completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")
print(f"Done: {last_action_result.get('done')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The white bar went from 4 to 2. The M square at (26-27, 32-33) is still inside the blue shape, and the other M squares haven't moved. The click at (27, 33) moved the M square that was already at (26-27, 32-33) to the same position (or it stayed there).
Wait, actually the M square was already at (26-27, 32-33) before this action. So clicking at (27, 33) again didn't move any M square, but the white bar still decreased from 4 to 2. This contradicts my earlier hypothesis that the white bar only decreases when an M square moves.
Let me reconsider. Maybe the white bar decreases by 2 for every valid action (board_changed=True), regardless of whether an M square moves.
But earlier, step 52 (click at 4, 31) didn't decrease the white bar (stayed at 10), and step 53 (click at 15, 31) decreased it from 10 to 8. Let me check if step 52 had board_changed=True or False.
Actually, I don't have that data anymore. Let me just continue and see if the level completes when the white bar reaches 0.
The white bar is at 2. I need 1 more valid action to reduce it to 0. Let me click somewhere to move an M square.
Actually, let me think about this more carefully. In level 1, the white bar reached 28 and the level was completed. In level 2, the white bar is at 2. If the target is 0, the level might complete on the next action.
But I'm not sure the target is 0. Let me just try one more action and see what happens.

sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# White bar is at 2. Let me try one more action to see if the level completes.
# Click at (27, 33) again to move an M square.

print("One more action to reduce white bar to 0...")
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 27, 'col': 33}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
